name: Szechwan Spiced Beef Shred B1
recipeIngredient: 2 Dry red chili peppers - finely ground (use seeds)
1 tbsp Chinese red wine vinegar or cider vinegar
1/2 cup Water OR clear chicken broth
2 tbsp Cornstarch mixed with 1 tbsp Water (to make paste)
recipeInstructions:
-------------------------
MARINADE
1. Mix together salt, sugar, and cornstarch.
2. Thinly slice the steak against the grain and cut into matchstick size strips.
3. Marinate for 2 to 4 hours.
4. Heat 4 tsp oil in a wok to 350°F. Add beef mixture and stir to separate pieces.
5. Blanch briskly until beef just loses its redness. Remove to a bowl.
6. Heat 2 tsp oil in the wok. Add ginger, garlic, scallions, and chili peppers. Stir-fry for about 10 seconds.
7. Stir in sherry, vinegar, and MSG (if desired). Cook until it bubbles gently.
8. Add water chestnuts, bamboo shoots, and green bell pepper. Return beef mixture to the wok and blend all together.
9. Pour in water or chicken broth. When it begins to boil, stir in the cornstarch mixture and continue stirring until the sauce thickens.
10. Add sesame oil, mix well, and serve.
Temperature (s): HOT
Effort: AVERAGE
Time: 04:00
